steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For $3x^2-5x-5=0$, the coefficients are $a=3$, $b=-5$, and $c=-5$. Substitute them into the quadratic formula.
$$x=\frac{-(-5)\pm\sqrt{(-5)^2-4(3)(-5)}}{2(3)}$$

steps.title: Choose the greater solution
steps.content:
Simplify the formula.
$$x=\frac{5\pm\sqrt{25+60}}{6}=\frac{5\pm\sqrt{85}}{6}$$
The greater solution uses the plus sign. The answer is B.
